name: skeleton-text
title: SkeletonText
description: A line-shaped placeholder for text content while loading. Use to reserve space for headings, single labels, or paragraphs of body copy until the real text arrives.

 *
 * Carbon's `SkeletonText` renders one or more line-shaped placeholders
 * for text. shadcn ships one generic `Skeleton` primitive тАФ sized via
 * Tailwind for line shapes (e.g. `<Skeleton className="h-4 w-full" />`).
 * For multi-line text, render multiple Skeletons in a vertical stack.
 * Canonical primitive lives at `skeleton/skeleton.shadcn.tsx`.
 */

export {Skeleton} from '../skeleton/skeleton.shadcn';

meta.docs:
# SkeletonText

A line-shaped placeholder for text content while loading. Use to reserve space for headings, single labels, or paragraphs of body copy until the real text arrives.

## When to use

- **Single line of body text** тАФ `<Skeleton className="h-4 w-full" />` (or a fixed width like `w-[180px]`).
- **Heading placeholder** тАФ `<Skeleton className="h-7 w-2/3" />` (taller, narrower than full body).
- **Multi-line paragraph** тАФ stack several `<Skeleton className="h-4 тАж" />` divs in a flex column; vary widths (`w-full`, `w-11/12`, `w-3/4`) for realism.
- **Last-line shorter** тАФ hard-code `w-3/4` or `w-2/3` on the final skeleton in a paragraph stack.
- **Composed loading row** тАФ pair with a `<Skeleton className="size-4" />` icon in a flex row.
- **Screen-reader announcement** тАФ wrap the loading group in `<div role="status" aria-busy="true" aria-label="Loading">`.

## When not to use

- **Square icon placeholders** тАФ use `SkeletonIcon` (same generic primitive sized as a square).
- **Per-component skeletons** ("input skeleton", "card skeleton") тАФ assemble from `<Skeleton>` rectangles to match the rendered shape.
- **Spinner-style indeterminate progress** тАФ use `Loading` / `Spinner`.

## Composition

- The wrapper re-exports the canonical `Skeleton` from `skeleton/`. There is no `paragraph`, `lineCount`, or `heading` prop тАФ render the layout yourself.
- Default animation is `animate-pulse` (opacity), not Carbon's shimmer.
- Default radius is `rounded-md`; for square corners (matching Carbon) add `className="rounded-none"`.
meta.migrationGuide:
# SkeletonText тАФ Carbon тЖТ shadcn migration guide ## Same primitive as SkeletonIcon Carbon's `SkeletonText` and `SkeletonIcon` are siblings in the same family тАФ `SkeletonText` is sized as a horizontal line, `SkeletonIcon` as a square. Both map to **shadcn's single generic `Skeleton`** primitive. For full background on Carbon's per-content-type skeleton family vs. shadcn's one-size-fits-all primitive, see the `skeleton-icon/migration_guide.md`. This guide focuses on the text-specific migration. ## Mapping | Carbon `SkeletonText` prop | shadcn | |---|---| | default (single line) | `` (or fixed width) | | `width="180px"` | `` | | `paragraph` + `lineCount={n}` | render `n` `` divs in a vertical flex stack; vary widths for realism | | `heading` | taller Skeleton: `` | | `lineCount` (without `paragraph`) | not directly supported; use `paragraph` semantics | | `className` | passes through; Tailwind on `` | ## Composition recipes **Single line** (default Carbon): ```tsx ``` **Multi-line paragraph** (matches Carbon ``): ```tsx
``` The varying widths (`w-full`, `w-11/12`, `w-3/4`) avoid the artificial-looking "perfect rectangle" of equal-width lines. **Heading** (matches Carbon ``): ```tsx ``` **Common "card row" pattern** (icon + label): ```tsx
``` **List of rows**: ```tsx
{Array.from({length: 5}).map((_, i) => (
))}
``` ## Carbon features missing in shadcn - **`paragraph` + `lineCount` props** тАФ Carbon renders the multi-line layout for you. shadcn: render multiple Skeletons yourself. - **`heading` prop** тАФ Carbon's taller line variant. shadcn: taller `h-N` class. - **Pre-baked variation in line widths** тАФ Carbon's `paragraph` mode renders the last line shorter. shadcn: vary `w-N` classes manually. - **Per-component skeletons** (``, ``, ``, etc.) тАФ Carbon ships dozens. shadcn: assemble them by composing `` divs to match the shape of the loading content. ## Migration checklist 1. Replace `` with `` (single line) or a fixed width. 2. Replace `` with a flex column of `n` Skeletons; vary widths for realism (e.g. `w-full`, `w-11/12`, `w-full`, `w-3/4`). 3. Replace `` with `` (or taller height to match your heading scale). 4. For `width="200px"`: `` (arbitrary value) or use a Tailwind size token (`w-48` = 192px). 5. For Carbon's automatic last-line-shorter behaviour in paragraphs: hard-code `w-3/4` or `w-2/3` on the last Skeleton in the stack. 6. If you used a per-component `` (e.g., ``, ``): there's no shadcn equivalent тАФ assemble the skeleton from `` rectangles to match the shape of the rendered component. 7. For announcing loading state to screen readers: wrap the skeleton group in `
`.
